108:   baseTtlMs: number,

110: ): number {

118: function filterByTime(items: NewsItem[], start?: string, end?: string) {

120:   const e = end ? new Date(end).getTime() : Infinity;
121:   return items.filter(x => {

123:     return t >= s && t <= e;

127: async function parseBySource(source: Source, http: HttpClient, cacheMode: 'prefer'|'bypass'|'only', overrideUrl?: string) {
128:   // Route social media sources to their platform parsers (no HTTP fetch)
129:   if (source.platform === 'reddit' || source.parser === 'reddit') {
130:     return await parseReddit(source);
131:   }
132:   if (source.platform === 'twitter' || source.parser === 'twitter') {
133:     return await parseTwitter(source);
134:   }
135: 
136:   const url = overrideUrl || source.url;
137:   const headers = source.headers || {};
138:   const res = await http.fetch(url, headers, cacheMode);
139:   if ('cached' in res && res.cached) {
140:     const items = (res.cache.items as any[]);
141:     return items as NewsItem[];
142:   }
143:   if (!('response' in res)) return [];
144:   let items: NewsItem[] = [];
145:   if (source.parser === 'ofac') {
146:     items = parseOfacHtml(source, res.response.bodyText);
147:   } else if (source.parser === 'ussf_cfc') {
148:     items = parseUssfcfcHtml(source, res.response.bodyText);
149:   } else if (source.parser === 'who_dons') {
150:     items = parseWhoJson(source, res.response.bodyText);
151:   } else if (source.parser === 'newslaundry') {
152:     items = parseNewslaundryHtml(source, res.response.bodyText);
153:   } else if (source.parser === 'semantic_scholar') {
154:     items = parseSemanticScholarJson(source, res.response.bodyText);
155:   } else if (source.parser === 'generic_html' && source.parserConfig?.selectors) {
156:     items = await parseWithSelectors(source, res.response.bodyText, source.parserConfig.selectors as any);
157:   } else {
158:     const ctype = (res.response.headers['content-type'] || '').toLowerCase();
159:     const body = res.response.bodyText;
160:     const looksXml = ctype.includes('xml') || ctype.includes('rss') || ctype.includes('atom') || /<rss[\s>/]/i.test(body) || /<feed[\s>/]/i.test(body);
161:     if (looksXml) {
162:       items = await parseRss(source, body, true);
163:     } else {
164:       if (ctype.includes('json') || /^[\[{]/.test(body.trim())) {
165:         const jitems = parseJsonFeed(source, body);
166:         if (jitems.length) { items = jitems; } else { items = []; }
167:       } else {
168:         try {
169:           const $ = loadHtml(body);
170:           const linkEl = $("link[rel='alternate'][type*='rss'], link[rel='alternate'][type*='atom']").first();
171:           const href = linkEl.attr('href');
172:           if (href) {
173:             const rssUrl = new URL(href, url).toString();
174:             const res2 = await http.fetch(rssUrl, headers, cacheMode);
175:             if ('response' in res2) {
176:               items = await parseRss(source, res2.response.bodyText, true);
177:             }
178:           } else {
179:             const auto = await autoParseHtml(source, body);
180:             items = auto.items;
181:           }
182:         } catch { items = []; }
183:       }
184:     }
185:   }
186:   await http.writeCache(url, items, (res as any).etag, (res as any).lastModified);
187:   return items;
188: }

190: export function filterByKeywords(items: NewsItem[], keywords: any = [], exclude: string[] = [], matchAll = false): NewsItem[] {
191:   if ((!keywords || (Array.isArray(keywords) && keywords.length === 0)) && (!exclude || exclude.length === 0)) return items;
192:   
193:   const exs = (exclude || []).map(k => k.toLowerCase());
194:   
195:   // Normalize keywords into clusters (each cluster is a set of synonyms)
196:   const clusters: string[][] = Array.isArray(keywords[0]) 
197:     ? keywords 
198:     : (keywords || []).map((k: string) => [k]);
199:     
200:   const normalizedClusters = clusters.map(c => c.map(k => k.toLowerCase()));
201: 
202:   return items.filter(it => {
203:     const text = `${it.title} ${it.content_snippet} ${it.link}`.toLowerCase();
204:     
205:     // Exclude if any excluded keyword matches
206:     if (exs.length && exs.some(e => text.includes(e))) return false;
207:     
208:     if (normalizedClusters.length === 0) return true;
209:     
210:     // For each cluster, at least one term must match
211:     const clusterMatches = normalizedClusters.map(cluster => 
212:       cluster.some(term => text.includes(term))
213:     );
214:     
215:     return matchAll ? clusterMatches.every(m => m) : clusterMatches.some(m => m);
216:   });
217: }

227:     result = result.filter(s => {

240:     result = result.filter(s =>

247: async function enrichArticlesFromLinks(items: NewsItem[], concurrency: number = 4, timeoutMs: number = 8000): Promise<NewsItem[]> {
248:   const toEnrich = items.filter(it =>
249:     it.link.includes('news.google.com') ||
250:     !it.content_snippet ||
251:     it.content_snippet.length < 100
252:   );
253:   if (!toEnrich.length) return items;
254: 
255:   const queue: Promise<void>[] = [];
256:   let active = 0;
257: 
258:   function processItem(item: NewsItem): Promise<void> {
259:     return (async () => {
260:       try {
261:         const res = await request(item.link, {
262:           method: 'GET',
263:           headers: { 'user-agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; IGS/1.0)' },
264:           headersTimeout: timeoutMs,
265:           bodyTimeout: timeoutMs,
266:           maxRedirections: 5,
267:         } as any);
268:         if (res.statusCode >= 200 && res.statusCode < 300) {
269:           const html = await res.body.text();
270:           const content = extractArticleContent(html);
271:           if (content.length > 100) {
272:             item.content_snippet = content.slice(0, 2000);
273:           }
274:         }
275:       } catch {
276:         // keep original snippet on failure
277:       }
278:     })();
279:   }
280: 
281:   for (const item of toEnrich) {
282:     const p = processItem(item).finally(() => { active--; });
283:     queue.push(p);
284:     active++;
285:     if (active >= concurrency) {
286:       await Promise.race(queue);
287:     }
288:   }
289:   await Promise.allSettled(queue);
290:   return items;
291: }

293: function batchSimilarNews(items: NewsItem[], threshold: number = 0.3): NewsItem[] {
294:   if (items.length <= 1) return items;
295: 
296:   const results: NewsItem[] = [];
297:   const used = new Set<number>();
298: 
299:   const getWordSet = (text: string) => {
300:     const words = text.toLowerCase()
301:       .replace(/[^\w\s]/g, ' ')
302:       .split(/\s+/)
303:       .filter(w => w.length > 2);
304:     return new Set(words);
305:   };
306: 
307:   for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
308:     if (used.has(i)) continue;
309: 
310:     const cluster = [i];
311:     const setI = getWordSet(items[i].title);
312: 
313:     for (let j = i + 1; j < items.length; j++) {
314:       if (used.has(j)) continue;
315: 
316:       const setJ = getWordSet(items[j].title);
317:       const intersection = new Set([...setI].filter(x => setJ.has(x)));
318:       const union = new Set([...setI, ...setJ]);
319:       const similarity = intersection.size / union.size;
320: 
321:       if (similarity >= threshold) {
322:         cluster.push(j);
323:       }
324:     }
325: 
326:     // Select the best representative for the cluster
327:     // Priority: Source Authority (simplified) > Recency
328:     const bestIdx = cluster.reduce((prev, curr) => {
329:       const a = items[prev];
330:       const b = items[curr];
331:       // Simple authority: favor specific high-quality IDs
332:       const authority = ['reuters', 'ap_world', 'ap_us', 'nytimes', 'wsj', 'bbc_world'].reduce((acc, id) => {
333:         if (a.source_name?.toLowerCase().includes(id) || a.id?.includes(id)) acc.a++;
334:         if (b.source_name?.toLowerCase().includes(id) || b.id?.includes(id)) acc.b++;
335:         return acc;
336:       }, { a: 0, b: 0 });

338:       if (authority.a !== authority.b) return authority.a > authority.b ? prev : curr;
339:       return new Date(b.pubDate).getTime() > new Date(a.pubDate).getTime() ? curr : prev;
340:     }, cluster[0]);
341: 
342:     results.push(items[bestIdx]);
343:     cluster.forEach(idx => used.add(idx));
344:   }
345: 
346:   return results;
347: }

2:  * Twitter/X API v2 utility with Nitter RSS fallback.
3:  *
4:  * Mode 1 (preferred): Twitter API v2 with Bearer Token auth
5:  *   - Reads `TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN` from environment

10:  *   - No authentication needed
11:  *   - Parses RSS feed for tweet text and timestamps
12:  *   - Tries multiple Nitter instances for reliability
13:  *   - Best-effort — may be unreliable
14:  *
15:  * @module
16:  */
17: 
18: import { request } from 'undici';
19: import { RateLimiter, platformRateLimitKey, TWITTER_PLATFORM_LIMIT } from './rate-limiter.js';
20: 
21: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
22: // Types
23: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24: 
25: export interface Tweet {
26:   id: string;
27:   text: string;
28:   author_id?: string;
29:   author_username?: string;
30:   author_name?: string;
31:   created_at: string;  // ISO string
32:   public_metrics?: {
33:     retweet_count: number;
34:     reply_count: number;
35:     like_count: number;
36:     quote_count: number;
37:   };
38:   referenced_tweets?: Array<{ type: string; id: string }>;
39:   attachments?: { media_keys: string[] };
40:   entities?: {
41:     urls?: Array<{ url: string; expanded_url: string; display_url: string }>;
42:     hashtags?: Array<{ tag: string }>;
43:     mentions?: Array<{ username: string }>;
44:   };
45:   source?: string;  // client used to post
46:   lang?: string;
47:   possibly_sensitive?: boolean;
48: }
49: 
50: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51: // Constants
52: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53: 
54: const TWITTER_API_BASE = 'https://api.twitter.com/2';
55: 
56: /** Default Nitter instances to try in order of reliability. */
57: const NITTER_INSTANCES: string[] = [
58:   'https://nitter.net',
59:   'https://nitter.privacydev.net',
60:   'https://nitter.1d4.us',
61:   'https://nitter.kavin.rocks',
62: ];
63: 
64: /** Tweet fields requested from the API v2. */
65: const TWEET_FIELDS = [
66:   'attachments',
67:   'author_id',
68:   'entities',
69:   'public_metrics',
70:   'referenced_tweets',
71:   'source',
72:   'lang',
73:   'possibly_sensitive',
74:   'created_at',
75: ].join(',');
76: 
77: const USER_FIELDS = ['username', 'name'].join(',');
78: 
79: const EXPANSIONS = ['author_id', 'attachments.media_keys'].join(',');
80: 
81: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82: // Rate limiter
83: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84: 
85: const twitterLimiter = new RateLimiter();
86: twitterLimiter.setLimit(platformRateLimitKey('twitter'), TWITTER_PLATFORM_LIMIT);
87: 
88: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
89: // Helpers
90: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
91: 
92: /**
93:  * Decode common HTML entities in text (no external deps).
94:  */
95: function decodeHtmlEntities(text: string): string {
96:   return text
97:     .replace(/&/g, '&')
98:     .replace(/</g, '<')
99:     .replace(/>/g, '>')
100:     .replace(/"/g, '"')
101:     .replace(/'/g, "'")
102:     .replace(/'/g, "'")
103:     .replace(/&#(\d+);/g, (_match, num) => String.fromCharCode(Number(num)));
104: }
105: 
106: /**
107:  * Minimal RSS feed parser (regex-based, no external deps).
108:  * Extracts title, link, pubDate, and description from <item> elements.
109:  */
110: function parseRSSFeed(xml: string): Array<{
111:   title: string;
112:   link: string;
113:   pubDate: string;
114:   description: string;
115: }> {
116:   const items: Array<{
117:     title: string;
118:     link: string;
119:     pubDate: string;
120:     description: string;
121:   }> = [];
122: 
123:   // Support both <item> and <entry> (Atom) feeds
124:   const itemRegex = /<(?:item|entry)>([\s\S]*?)<\/(?:item|entry)>/gi;
125:   let match: RegExpExecArray | null;
126: 
127:   while ((match = itemRegex.exec(xml)) !== null) {
128:     const content = match[1];
129: 
130:     const extract = (tag: string): string => {
131:       const re = new RegExp(`<${tag}(?:\\s[^>]*)?>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/${tag}>`, 'i');
132:       const m = re.exec(content);
133:       return m ? m[1].trim() : '';
134:     };
135: 
136:     const extractFromCdata = (tag: string): string => {
137:       const cdataRe = new RegExp(
138:         `<${tag}(?:\\s[^>]*)?><!\\[CDATA\\[([\\s\\S]*?)\\]\\]><\\/${tag}>`,
139:         'i',
140:       );
141:       const m = cdataRe.exec(content);
142:       if (m) return m[1].trim();
143:       return extract(tag);
144:     };
145: 
146:     const title = decodeHtmlEntities(extract('title'));
147:     const link = extract('link') || extract('id');  // Atom uses <id>
148:     const pubDate = extract('pubDate') || extract('published') || extract('updated');
149:     const description = decodeHtmlEntities(extractFromCdata('description') || extract('description') || extract('content') || title);
150: 
151:     if (title || link) {
152:       items.push({ title, link, pubDate, description });
153:     }
154:   }
155: 
156:   return items;
157: }
158: 
159: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
160: // Mode 1: Twitter API v2
161: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
162: 
163: /**
164:  * Resolve a @username to a numeric user ID via the Twitter API v2.
165:  */
166: async function resolveUserId(
167:   username: string,
168:   bearerToken: string,
169: ): Promise<string | null> {
170:   try {
171:     const url = `${TWITTER_API_BASE}/users/by/username/${encodeURIComponent(username)}`;
172:     const res = await request(url, {
173:       method: 'GET',
174:       headers: {
175:         Authorization: `Bearer ${bearerToken}`,
176:         Accept: 'application/json',
177:       },
178:       headersTimeout: 10_000,
179:       bodyTimeout: 10_000,
180:     });
181: 
182:     if (res.statusCode !== 200) {
183:       console.error(`Twitter user resolution failed: ${res.statusCode}`);
184:       return null;
185:     }
186: 
187:     const body: any = JSON.parse(await res.body.text());
188:     return body.data?.id ?? null;
189:   } catch (err) {
190:     console.error('Twitter user resolution error:', err);
191:     return null;
192:   }
193: }
194: 
195: /**
196:  * Fetch tweets via the official Twitter API v2 using Bearer Token auth.
197:  */
198: async function fetchViaAPI(
199:   username: string,
200:   maxResults: number,
201: ): Promise<Tweet[]> {
202:   const bearerToken = process.env.TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN;
203:   if (!bearerToken) {
204:     return [];
205:   }
206: 
207:   const userId = await resolveUserId(username, bearerToken);
208:   if (!userId) {
209:     return [];
210:   }
211: 
212:   const actualMax = Math.min(maxResults, 100); // API limit per request
213: 
214:   const url = new URL(`${TWITTER_API_BASE}/users/${userId}/tweets`);
215:   url.searchParams.set('max_results', String(actualMax));
216:   url.searchParams.set('tweet.fields', TWEET_FIELDS);
217:   url.searchParams.set('user.fields', USER_FIELDS);
218:   url.searchParams.set('expansions', EXPANSIONS);
219: 
220:   try {
221:     await twitterLimiter.wait(platformRateLimitKey('twitter'), 30_000);
222: 
223:     const res = await request(url.toString(), {
224:       method: 'GET',
225:       headers: {
226:         Authorization: `Bearer ${bearerToken}`,
227:         Accept: 'application/json',
228:       },
229:       headersTimeout: 15_000,
230:       bodyTimeout: 15_000,
231:     });
232: 
233:     if (res.statusCode !== 200) {
234:       console.error(`Twitter API returned ${res.statusCode}`);
235:       return [];
236:     }
237: 
238:     const body: any = JSON.parse(await res.body.text());
239:     const tweets: Tweet[] = body.data ?? [];
240: 
241:     // Enrich tweets with author info from the "includes" section
242:     if (body.includes?.users) {
243:       const userMap = new Map<string, { username: string; name: string }>();
244:       for (const user of body.includes.users) {
245:         userMap.set(user.id, { username: user.username, name: user.name });
246:       }
247:       for (const tweet of tweets) {
248:         if (tweet.author_id && userMap.has(tweet.author_id)) {
249:           const user = userMap.get(tweet.author_id)!;
250:           tweet.author_username = user.username;
251:           tweet.author_name = user.name;
252:         }
253:       }
254:     }
255: 
256:     return tweets;
257:   } catch (err) {
258:     console.error('Twitter API fetch error:', err);
259:     return [];
260:   }
261: }
262: 
263: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
264: // Mode 2: Nitter RSS fallback
265: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
266: 
267: /**
268:  * Fetch tweets by scraping a Nitter RSS feed (no auth required).
269:  *
270:  * Tries multiple Nitter instances in order. Returns as soon as one
271:  * returns at least one tweet.
272:  */
273: async function fetchViaNitter(
274:   username: string,
275:   maxResults: number,
276: ): Promise<Tweet[]> {
277:   let lastError: Error | null = null;
278: 
279:   for (const instance of NITTER_INSTANCES) {
280:     try {
281:       const url = `${instance}/${encodeURIComponent(username)}/rss`;
282:       const res = await request(url, {
283:         method: 'GET',
284:         headers: {
285:           'User-Agent':
286:             'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; IGS/1.0; +https://github.com/igs-mcp)',
287:           Accept: 'application/rss+xml, application/xml, text/xml',
288:         },
289:         headersTimeout: 10_000,
290:         bodyTimeout: 10_000,
291:       });
292: 
293:       if (res.statusCode !== 200) {
294:         console.error(`Nitter instance ${instance} returned ${res.statusCode}`);
295:         continue;
296:       }
297: 
298:       const xml = await res.body.text();
299:       const entries = parseRSSFeed(xml);
300: 
301:       const tweets: Tweet[] = [];
302:       for (const entry of entries) {
303:         if (tweets.length >= maxResults) break;
304: 
305:         // Extract tweet ID from the status link
306:         const tweetIdMatch = entry.link.match(/\/status\/(\d+)/);
307:         const tweetId = tweetIdMatch
308:           ? tweetIdMatch[1]
309:           : `nitter_${Date.now()}_${tweets.length}`;
310: 
311:         // Nitter <title> is usually "@{username} on Twitter: \"{text}\""
312:         // Prefer <description> which holds the full tweet body
313:         const text = entry.description || entry.title;
314: 
315:         tweets.push({
316:           id: tweetId,
317:           text,
318:           author_username: username,
319:           created_at: entry.pubDate
320:             ? new Date(entry.pubDate).toISOString()
321:             : new Date().toISOString(),
322:         });
323:       }
324: 
325:       if (tweets.length > 0) {
326:         return tweets;
327:       }
328:     } catch (err) {
329:       lastError = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err));
330:       console.error(`Nitter instance ${instance} failed:`, err);
331:       // Try next instance
332:     }
333:   }
334: 
335:   if (lastError) {
336:     console.error('All Nitter instances failed:', lastError);
337:   }
338:   return [];
339: }
340: 
341: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
342: // TwitterClient
343: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
344: 
345: /**
346:  * Client for fetching tweets from Twitter/X.
347:  *
348:  * Automatically selects the best available mode:
349:  *   1. Twitter API v2 (if `TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN` env var is set)
350:  *   2. Nitter RSS scraping (no auth needed, best-effort)
351:  */
352: export class TwitterClient {
353:   /**
354:    * Fetch the most recent tweets from a user's timeline.
355:    *
356:    * @param username    Twitter handle (with or without leading `@`)
357:    * @param maxResults  Max tweets to return (default 20, max 100)
358:    */
359:   async getUserTimeline(
360:     username: string,
361:     maxResults: number = 20,
362:   ): Promise<Tweet[]> {
363:     const sanitized = username.replace(/^@/, '').trim().toLowerCase();
364:     if (!sanitized) {
365:       return [];
366:     }
367: 
368:     const limit = Math.min(Math.max(1, maxResults), 100);
369:     const bearerToken = process.env.TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN;
370: 
371:     // Mode 1: API (preferred)
372:     if (bearerToken) {
373:       const tweets = await fetchViaAPI(sanitized, limit);
374:       if (tweets.length > 0) {
375:         return tweets;
376:       }
377:       console.warn(
378:         `Twitter API returned 0 tweets for @${sanitized}, falling back to Nitter...`,
379:       );
380:     }
381: 
382:     // Mode 2: Nitter fallback
383:     const nitterTweets = await fetchViaNitter(sanitized, limit);
384:     return nitterTweets;
385:   }
386: }
387: 
388: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
389: // Normalization
390: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
391: 
392: /**
393:  * Normalize a {@link Tweet} into a {@link import('./types/news.js').NewsItem}
394:  * shape suitable for IGS pipelines.
395:  *
396:  * @param tweet      The tweet to normalize.
397:  * @param sourceName Source name (e.g. `"@elonmusk"` or `"twitter (nitter)"`).
398:  * @param poolId     Target pool id (e.g. `"GLOBAL_TECH_CYBER"`).
399:  */
400: export function normalizeTweet(
401:   tweet: Tweet,
402:   sourceName: string,
403:   poolId: string,
404: ) {
405:   const tweetUrl = tweet.author_username
406:     ? `https://twitter.com/${tweet.author_username}/status/${tweet.id}`
407:     : `https://twitter.com/i/web/status/${tweet.id}`;
408: 
409:   // Build a meaningful content snippet
410:   const snippetParts: string[] = [tweet.text];
411:   if (tweet.entities?.urls && tweet.entities.urls.length > 0) {
412:     const firstUrl = tweet.entities.urls[0];
413:     if (firstUrl.expanded_url) {
414:       snippetParts.push(` ${firstUrl.expanded_url}`);
415:     }
416:   }
417:   const contentSnippet = snippetParts.join('').slice(0, 600);
418: 
419:   // Extract first media URL if present (image links in entities)
420:   let mediaUrl: string | undefined;
421:   if (tweet.entities?.urls && tweet.entities.urls.length > 0) {
422:     const firstUrl = tweet.entities.urls[0];
423:     if (
424:       firstUrl.expanded_url &&
425:       /\.(?:jpg|jpeg|png|gif|webp)(?:\?|$)/i.test(firstUrl.expanded_url)
426:     ) {
427:       mediaUrl = firstUrl.expanded_url;
428:     }
429:   }
430: 
431:   return {
432:     id: `tweet_${tweet.id}`,
433:     title: tweet.text.slice(0, 200),
434:     link: tweetUrl,
435:     pubDate: tweet.created_at,
436:     source_name: sourceName,
437:     pool_id: poolId,
438:     content_snippet: contentSnippet,
439:     author: tweet.author_username,
440:     media_url: mediaUrl,
441:   };
442: }

(End of file - total 442 lines)

1: /**
2:  * Centralized rate limiter for API request throttling.
3:  *
4:  * Supports two modes:
5:  * 1. Per-source interval limiting (based on SourceRateLimit.interval_seconds)
6:  * 2. Per-platform aggregate quota (e.g., 60 req/min for Reddit)
7:  *
8:  * Uses sliding-window counters with in-memory Map storage.
9:  * No external dependencies required.
10:  *
11:  * @module
12:  */
13: 
14: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
15: // Types & Interfaces
16: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17: 
18: export interface RateLimitConfig {
19:   /** Maximum number of requests allowed in the sliding window */
20:   maxRequests: number;
21:   /** Sliding window duration in milliseconds */
22:   windowMs: number;
23: }
24: 
25: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26: // Error
27: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28: 
29: /**
30:  * Thrown when a rate-limited operation times out waiting for a slot.
31:  */
32: export class RateLimitError extends Error {
33:   public readonly key: string;
34:   public readonly retryAfterMs: number;
35: 
36:   constructor(key: string, retryAfterMs: number, message?: string) {
37:     super(message ?? `Rate limit exceeded for key "${key}". Retry after ${retryAfterMs}ms.`);
38:     this.name = 'RateLimitError';
39:     this.key = key;
40:     this.retryAfterMs = retryAfterMs;
41:   }
42: }
43: 
44: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
45: // Default presets
46: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
47: 
48: /** Reddit: 60 requests per 60 seconds */
49: export const REDDIT_PLATFORM_LIMIT: RateLimitConfig = {
50:   maxRequests: 60,
51:   windowMs: 60_000,
52: };
53: 
54: /** Twitter/X: 450 requests per 15 minutes */
55: export const TWITTER_PLATFORM_LIMIT: RateLimitConfig = {
56:   maxRequests: 450,
57:   windowMs: 900_000,
58: };
59: 
60: /** Default interval between source fetches: 5 minutes */
61: export const DEFAULT_SOURCE_INTERVAL_MS: number = 300_000;
62: 
63: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64: // Key helpers
65: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
66: 
67: /**
68:  * Build a rate-limiter key for a specific source.
69:  * Used with SourceRateLimit.interval_seconds.
70:  */
71: export function sourceRateLimitKey(sourceId: string): string {
72:   return `source:${sourceId}`;
73: }
74: 
75: /**
76:  * Build a rate-limiter key for an entire platform.
77:  * Used with platform-wide quotas (e.g., Reddit, Twitter).
78:  */
79: export function platformRateLimitKey(platform: string): string {
80:   return `platform:${platform}`;
81: }
82: 
83: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84: // RateLimiter
85: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86: 
87: /**
88:  * Sliding-window rate limiter.
89:  *
90:  * ```ts
91:  * const limiter = new RateLimiter();
92:  * limiter.setLimit(sourceRateLimitKey('my-source'), {
93:  *   maxRequests: 1,
94:  *   windowMs: 300_000,
95:  * });
96:  *
97:  * await limiter.wait(sourceRateLimitKey('my-source'), 30_000);
98:  * // ... make request ...
99:  * ```
100:  */
101: export class RateLimiter {
102:   /** key → sorted array of request timestamps (most recent last) */
103:   private limits: Map<string, number[]> = new Map();
104:   /** key → configuration */
105:   private configs: Map<string, RateLimitConfig> = new Map();
106: 
107:   constructor() {}
108: 
109:   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
110:   // Public API
111:   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
112: 
113:   /**
114:    * Register or update a rate limit configuration for a key.
115:    */
116:   setLimit(key: string, config: RateLimitConfig): void {
117:     this.configs.set(key, { ...config });
118:   }
119: 
120:   /**
121:    * Check whether a request for `key` is allowed **right now**.
122:    *
123:    * @returns `true` if the request would be accepted (slot available).
124:    *          `true` if no limit has been configured for this key.
125:    */
126:   check(key: string): boolean {
127:     const config = this.configs.get(key);
128:     if (!config) return true;
129: 
130:     this.prune(key);
131: 
132:     const timestamps = this.limits.get(key);
133:     return timestamps === undefined || timestamps.length < config.maxRequests;
134:   }
135: 
136:   /**
137:    * Wait until a slot becomes available for `key`, then record the request.
138:    *
139:    * Polls every 100 ms. Throws {@link RateLimitError} if the timeout
140:    * is exceeded.
141:    *
142:    * @param key       The rate-limit key.
143:    * @param timeoutMs Max wait time in ms (default 30 000).
144:    */
145:   async wait(key: string, timeoutMs: number = 30_000): Promise<void> {
146:     const config = this.configs.get(key);
147:     if (!config) return;
148: 
149:     const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
150: 
151:     while (true) {
152:       if (this.check(key)) {
153:         const timestamps = this.limits.get(key) ?? [];
154:         timestamps.push(Date.now());
155:         this.limits.set(key, timestamps);
156:         return;
157:       }
158: 
159:       if (Date.now() >= deadline) {
160:         const retryAfterMs = this.timeUntilNext(key);
161:         throw new RateLimitError(key, retryAfterMs);
162:       }
163: 
164:       await new Promise<void>((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
165:     }
166:   }
167: 
168:   /**
169:    * Number of requests remaining in the current sliding window for `key`.
170:    *
171:    * @returns Remaining slots, or `Infinity` if no limit is configured.
172:    */
173:   remaining(key: string): number {
174:     const config = this.configs.get(key);
175:     if (!config) return Infinity;
176: 
177:     this.prune(key);
178:     const timestamps = this.limits.get(key);
179:     return timestamps ? Math.max(0, config.maxRequests - timestamps.length) : config.maxRequests;
180:   }
181: 
182:   /**
183:    * Milliseconds until the next slot becomes available for `key`.
184:    *
185:    * @returns `0` if a slot is already available, or positive ms otherwise.
186:    */
187:   timeUntilNext(key: string): number {
188:     const config = this.configs.get(key);
189:     if (!config) return 0;
190: 
191:     this.prune(key);
192:     const timestamps = this.limits.get(key);
193: 
194:     if (!timestamps || timestamps.length < config.maxRequests) return 0;
195: 
196:     const oldest = timestamps[0];
197:     const remaining = config.windowMs - (Date.now() - oldest);
198:     return Math.max(0, remaining);
199:   }
200: 
201:   /**
202:    * Reset all rate-limit state and configuration for `key`.
203:    */
204:   reset(key: string): void {
205:     this.limits.delete(key);
206:     this.configs.delete(key);
207:   }
208: 
209:   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
210:   // Private
211:   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
212: 
213:   private prune(key: string): void {
214:     const config = this.configs.get(key);
215:     if (!config) return;
216: 
217:     const now = Date.now();
218:     const cutoff = now - config.windowMs;
219:     const timestamps = this.limits.get(key);
220:     if (!timestamps || timestamps.length === 0) return;
221: 
222:     const firstValid = lowerBound(timestamps, cutoff + 1);
223: 
224:     if (firstValid === 0) return;
225:     if (firstValid >= timestamps.length) {
226:       this.limits.delete(key);
227:       return;
228:     }
229: 
230:     this.limits.set(key, timestamps.slice(firstValid));
231:   }
232: }
233: 
234: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
235: // Helpers
236: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
237: 
238: /**
239:  * Return the leftmost index in a **sorted** array where `value` could be
240:  * inserted while maintaining order (i.e. first element >= value).
241:  *
242:  * Equivalent to `std::lower_bound` / Python `bisect_left`.
243:  */
244: function lowerBound(arr: number[], value: number): number {
245:   let lo = 0;
246:   let hi = arr.length;
247:   while (lo < hi) {
248:     const mid = (lo + hi) >>> 1;
249:     if (arr[mid] < value) lo = mid + 1;
250:     else hi = mid;
251:   }
252:   return lo;
253: }

1: /**
2:  * Tier Validator Utility
3:  *
4:  * Validates and manages source quality tiers (Tier 1/2/3).
5:  * Tiers are used to prioritize sources during dedup and to adjust cache policies.
6:  *
7:  * All functions are pure — no side effects, no dependencies on other IGS modules.
8:  */
9: 
10: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
11: // Types
12: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13: 
14: export type Tier = 1 | 2 | 3;
15: 
16: export interface TierConfig {
17:   value: Tier;
18:   label: string;
19:   description: string;
20:   /** Default cache TTL multiplier (higher = cache longer, tier 1 is most authoritative) */
21:   cacheTtlMultiplier: number;
22:   /** Sources at this tier are considered authoritative for dedup priority */
23:   isAuthoritative: boolean;
24: }
25: 
26: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
27: // Configuration
28: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
29: 
30: export const TIER_CONFIG: Record<Tier, TierConfig> = {
31:   1: {
32:     value: 1,
33:     label: 'Primary / Official',
34:     description:
35:       'Official sources, peer-reviewed journals, government agencies, primary data',
36:     cacheTtlMultiplier: 2.0,
37:     isAuthoritative: true,
38:   },
39:   2: {
40:     value: 2,
41:     label: 'Major Outlet',
42:     description:
43:       'Established news outlets, major think tanks, reputable independent media',
44:     cacheTtlMultiplier: 1.0,
45:     isAuthoritative: false,
46:   },
47:   3: {
48:     value: 3,
49:     label: 'Blog / Social / Niche',
50:     description:
51:       'Blogs, social media, niche publications, unverified sources, Reddit, Twitter',
52:     cacheTtlMultiplier: 0.5,
53:     isAuthoritative: false,
54:   },
55: };
56: 
57: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
58: // Validation
59: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60: 
61: const VALID_TIERS = new Set<Tier>([1, 2, 3]);
62: 
63: /**
64:  * Validate that a tier value is 1, 2, or 3.
65:  * Accepts numbers and their string representations.
66:  * Returns the tier if valid, or undefined.
67:  */
68: export function validateTier(value: unknown): Tier | undefined {
69:   if (typeof value === 'number' && VALID_TIERS.has(value as Tier)) {
70:     return value as Tier;
71:   }
72:   if (typeof value === 'string') {
73:     const num = Number(value);
74:     if (Number.isInteger(num) && VALID_TIERS.has(num as Tier)) {
75:       return num as Tier;
76:     }
77:   }
78:   return undefined;
79: }
80: 
81: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82: // Lookup helpers
83: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84: 
85: /**
86:  * Get human-readable label for a tier.
87:  * Returns 'Unknown Tier' if invalid.
88:  */
89: export function getTierLabel(tier: Tier): string {
90:   const config = TIER_CONFIG[tier];
91:   return config ? config.label : 'Unknown Tier';
92: }
93: 
94: /**
95:  * Check if a source with given tier is considered reliable for dedup priority.
96:  * Tier 1 sources win in dedup conflicts. Tier 3 never wins.
97:  */
98: export function isSourceReliable(tier?: Tier): boolean {
99:   if (tier === undefined) return false;
100:   const config = TIER_CONFIG[tier];
101:   return config ? config.isAuthoritative : false;
102: }
103: 
104: /**
105:  * Get cache TTL multiplier for a tier.
106:  * Tier 1 = 2x (cache longer, authoritative content changes slowly)
107:  * Tier 2 = 1x (normal)
108:  * Tier 3 = 0.5x (social media changes fast, cache less)
109:  */
110: export function getTierCacheMultiplier(tier?: Tier): number {
111:   if (tier === undefined) return 1.0;
112:   const config = TIER_CONFIG[tier];
113:   return config ? config.cacheTtlMultiplier : 1.0;
114: }

1: #!/usr/bin/env bun
2: /**
3:  * validate-sources.ts — Pre-validation script for RSS/JSON feeds and other sources.
4:  *
5:  * Tests each source URL for:
6:  *   1. HTTP 200 status
7:  *   2. Content-Type header inspection
8:  *   3. Parsing simulation (where possible)
9:  *
10:  * Usage:
11:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
12:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser> [--url <url2> --parser <parser2> ...]
13:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
14:  *
15:  * YAML file format (matching sources.yml):
16:  *   sources:
17:  *     - url: https://example.com/feed
18:  *       parser: rss
19:  *       name: optional label
20:  *
21:  * Or a flat list:
22:  *   - url: https://example.com/feed
23:  *     parser: rss
24:  *
25:  * Exit code: 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
26:  */
27: 
28: import RSSParser from 'rss-parser';
29: import { request } from 'undici';
30: import yaml from 'js-yaml';
31: import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
32: 
33: const rssParser = new RSSParser({ timeout: 15000 });
34: 
35: // ── Types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: 
37: interface SourceEntry {
38:   url: string;
39:   parser: string;
40:   name?: string;
41: }
42: 
43: interface ValidationResult {
44:   url: string;
45:   parser: string;
46:   status: 'ok' | 'fail' | 'partial';
47:   items_count: number;
48:   error?: string;
49:   content_type?: string;
50:   feed_type?: string;
51:   sample_title?: string;
52: }
53: 
54: interface Summary {
55:   total: number;
56:   passed: number;
57:   failed: number;
58:   partial: number;
59: }
60: 
61: interface Report {
62:   summary: Summary;
63:   results: ValidationResult[];
64: }
65: 
66: // ── CLI argument parsing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67: 
68: function parseArgs(): { entries: SourceEntry[]; filePath: string | null } {
69:   const args = process.argv.slice(2);
70:   const entries: SourceEntry[] = [];
71:   let filePath: string | null = null;
72: 
73:   const fileIndex = args.indexOf('--file');
74:   if (fileIndex !== -1 && fileIndex + 1 < args.length) {
75:     filePath = args[fileIndex + 1];
76:   }
77: 
78:   for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
79:     if (args[i] === '--url' && i + 1 < args.length) {
80:       const url = args[i + 1];
81:       if (!url.startsWith('http://') && !url.startsWith('https://')) {
82:         console.error(`Invalid URL: ${url}`);
83:         process.exit(1);
84:       }
85:       let parser = 'rss';
86:       for (let j = i + 2; j < args.length; j++) {
87:         if (args[j] === '--url' || args[j] === '--file') break;
88:         if (args[j] === '--parser' && j + 1 < args.length) {
89:           parser = args[j + 1];
90:           break;
91:         }
92:       }
93:       entries.push({ url, parser });
94:     }
95:   }
96: 
97:   return { entries, filePath };
98: }
99: 
100: async function loadSourcesFromYaml(filePath: string): Promise<SourceEntry[]> {
101:   const raw = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
102:   const data = yaml.load(raw) as any;
103: 
104:   if (!data) throw new Error(`Empty or invalid YAML file: ${filePath}`);
105: 
106:   const list: any[] = data.sources ?? (Array.isArray(data) ? data : null);
107:   if (!list) throw new Error(`YAML file must have a "sources" key or be a flat list`);
108: 
109:   return list.map((entry: any, idx: number) => {
110:     if (!entry.url) throw new Error(`Entry ${idx} is missing "url"`);
111:     if (!entry.parser) throw new Error(`Entry ${idx} is missing "parser"`);
112:     return { url: entry.url, parser: entry.parser, name: entry.name };
113:   });
114: }
115: 
116: // ── Feed type detection helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────
117: 
118: function detectFeedType(body: string, contentType: string): string {
119:   const trimmed = body.trim();
120:   if (trimmed.startsWith('<?xml') || trimmed.startsWith('<rss') || trimmed.startsWith('<feed')) {
121:     if (/<rss[\s>/]/i.test(trimmed)) return 'rss';
122:     if (/<feed[\s>/]/i.test(trimmed)) return 'atom';
123:     if (contentType.includes('xml')) return 'xml';
124:     return 'xml';
125:   }
126:   if (trimmed.startsWith('{') || trimmed.startsWith('[')) {
127:     try {
128:       const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed);
129:       if (parsed?.version && parsed?.items) return 'json_feed';
130:       if (parsed?.items) return 'json_feed_like';
131:       return 'json';
132:     } catch {
133:       return 'json_like';
134:     }
135:   }
136:   if (trimmed.startsWith('%PDF')) return 'pdf';
137:   return 'html';
138: }
139: 
140: // ── Validation per parser type ───────────────────────────────────────────────
141: 
142: async function validateSource(entry: SourceEntry): Promise<ValidationResult> {
143:   const { url, parser } = entry;
144:   const result: ValidationResult = {
145:     url,
146:     parser,
147:     status: 'fail',
148:     items_count: 0,
149:   };
150: 
151:   let body: string;
152:   let statusCode: number;
153:   let rawContentType: string;
154: 
155:   try {
156:     const controller = new AbortController();
157:     const timeout = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 15000);
158: 
159:     const resp = await request(url, {
160:       method: 'GET',
161:       headers: {
162:         'User-Agent': 'IGS-Validator/1.0 (+https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp)',
163:         Accept: 'application/rss+xml, application/json, text/html, application/xml, application/pdf, */*',
164:       },
165:       signal: controller.signal,
166:     });
167: 
168:     clearTimeout(timeout);
169: 
170:     statusCode = resp.statusCode;
171:     rawContentType = (resp.headers['content-type'] as string) || '';
172:     result.content_type = rawContentType;
173: 
174:     if (statusCode !== 200) {
175:       result.status = 'fail';
176:       result.error = `HTTP ${statusCode}`;
177:       return result;
178:     }
179: 
180:     const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
181:     for await (const chunk of resp.body) {
182:       chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(chunk));
183:     }
184:     body = Buffer.concat(chunks).toString('utf-8');
185: 
186:     if (body.length === 0) {
187:       result.status = 'fail';
188:       result.error = 'Empty response body';
189:       return result;
190:     }
191:   } catch (err: any) {
192:     result.status = 'fail';
193:     if (err.name === 'AbortError') {
194:       result.error = 'timeout (15s)';
195:     } else if (err.code === 'ENOTFOUND') {
196:       result.error = `DNS resolution failed: ${err.message}`;
197:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNREFUSED') {
198:       result.error = `Connection refused: ${err.message}`;
199:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNRESET') {
200:       result.error = `Connection reset: ${err.message}`;
201:     } else if (err.code === 'UND_ERR_HEADERS_TIMEOUT') {
202:       result.error = 'Headers timeout';
203:     } else if (err.type === 'aborted' || err.message?.includes('abort')) {
204:       result.error = 'timeout (15s)';
205:     } else {
206:       result.error = err.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(err);
207:     }
208:     return result;
209:   }
210: 
211:   result.feed_type = detectFeedType(body, rawContentType);
212: 
213:   try {
214:     await simulateParse(parser, body, result);
215:   } catch (parseErr: any) {
216:     result.status = 'fail';
217:     result.error = `Parse error: ${parseErr.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(parseErr)}`;
218:   }
219: 
220:   return result;
221: }
222: 
223: async function simulateParse(
224:   parser: string,
225:   body: string,
226:   result: ValidationResult,
227: ): Promise<void> {
228:   switch (parser) {
229:     case 'rss': {
230:       let feed;
231:       try {
232:         feed = await rssParser.parseString(body);
233:       } catch {
234:         // rss-parser may also handle Atom; if it fails, try as raw XML
235:         feed = { items: [] };
236:       }
237:       const items = feed.items || [];
238:       result.items_count = items.length;
239: 
240:       if (items.length > 0) {
241:         result.status = 'ok';
242:         result.sample_title = items[0]?.title?.slice(0, 120);
243:       } else {
244:         // Check if body at least looks like valid RSS/XML
245:         if (body.includes('<item>') || body.includes('<entry>') || body.includes('<rss') || body.includes('<feed')) {
246:           result.status = 'partial';
247:           result.error = 'Feed parsed but 0 items';
248:         } else {
249:           result.status = 'fail';
250:           result.error = 'Content does not appear to be RSS/Atom XML';
251:         }
252:       }
253:       break;
254:     }
255: 
256:     case 'json_feed': {
257:       let data: any;
258:       try {
259:         data = JSON.parse(body);
260:       } catch {
261:         result.status = 'fail';
262:         result.error = 'Invalid JSON';
263:         return;
264:       }
265: 
266:       const items = Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items
267:         : Array.isArray(data) ? data
268:         : Array.isArray(data?.value) ? data.value
269:         : [];
270: 
271:       result.items_count = items.length;
272: 
273:       const hasVersion = typeof data?.version === 'string' || data?.version !== undefined;
274:       const hasTitle = typeof data?.title === 'string';
275: 
276:       if (items.length > 0) {
277:         result.status = 'ok';
278:         result.sample_title = items[0]?.title || items[0]?.url || '(no title)';
279:         result.sample_title = String(result.sample_title).slice(0, 120);
280:       } else if (hasVersion || hasTitle) {
281:         result.status = 'partial';
282:         result.error = 'JSON feed structure recognized but 0 items';
283:       } else if (Array.isArray(data) && data.length > 0) {
284:         // Could be an array of items (some APIs return this)
285:         result.status = 'ok';
286:         result.items_count = data.length;
287:         result.sample_title = String(data[0]?.title || data[0]?.url || '').slice(0, 120);
288:       } else {
289:         result.status = 'fail';
290:         result.error = 'JSON does not match feed structure (missing version/items)';
291:       }
292:       break;
293:     }
294: 
295:     case 'generic_html':
296:     case 'article_content': {
297:       const stripped = body.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim();
298:       if (stripped.length > 0) {
299:         result.status = 'partial';
300:         result.items_count = 0;
301:         result.feed_type = result.feed_type || 'html';
302:       } else {
303:         result.status = 'fail';
304:         result.error = 'Empty or whitespace-only HTML body';
305:       }
306:       break;
307:     }
308: 
309:     case 'sitemap': {
310:       const trimmed = body.trim();
311:       if (trimmed.includes('<urlset') || trimmed.includes('<sitemapindex')) {
312:         const locMatches = body.match(/<loc>/g);
313:         result.items_count = locMatches ? locMatches.length : 0;
314:         result.status = result.items_count > 0 ? 'ok' : 'partial';
315:         if (result.items_count === 0) result.error = 'Sitemap parsed but 0 URLs found';
316:       } else if (trimmed.startsWith('<?xml') || trimmed.startsWith('<')) {
317:         result.status = 'partial';
318:         result.items_count = 0;
319:         result.error = 'XML content but not a recognized sitemap format';
320:       } else {
321:         result.status = 'fail';
322:         result.error = 'Not a sitemap XML';
323:       }
324:       break;
325:     }
326: 
327:     case 'ofac':
328:     case 'ussf_cfc':
329:     case 'newslaundry': {
330:       const stripped = body.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim();
331:       if (stripped.length > 100) {
332:         result.status = 'partial';
333:         result.items_count = 0;
334:       } else {
335:         result.status = 'fail';
336:         result.error = `Body too short for ${parser} parser (${stripped.length} chars text)`;
337:       }
338:       break;
339:     }
340: 
341:     case 'who_dons':
342:     case 'semantic_scholar': {
343:       try {
344:         const data = JSON.parse(body);
345:         result.status = 'partial';
346:         result.items_count = Array.isArray(data) ? data.length
347:           : Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items.length
348:           : Array.isArray(data?.results) ? data.results.length
349:           : 0;
350:         if (result.items_count === 0) result.error = 'JSON parsed but 0 items found in expected fields';
351:       } catch {
352:         result.status = 'fail';
353:         result.error = 'Invalid JSON for parser type';
354:       }
355:       break;
356:     }
357: 
358:     case 'pdf-extractor':
359:     case 'pdf-to-markdown': {
360:       if (body.startsWith('%PDF') || body.charCodeAt(0) === 0x25) {
361:         result.status = 'partial';
362:         result.items_count = 0;
363:         result.feed_type = 'pdf';
364:       } else {
365:         result.status = 'fail';
366:         result.error = 'Not a PDF file (missing %PDF header)';
367:       }
368:       break;
369:     }
370: 
371:     default: {
372:       result.status = 'partial';
373:       result.items_count = 0;
374:       result.error = `Unknown parser type "${parser}" — only connectivity verified`;
375:       break;
376:     }
377:   }
378: }
379: 
380: // ── Report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
381: 
382: function printReport(results: ValidationResult[]): void {
383:   const summary: Summary = { total: 0, passed: 0, failed: 0, partial: 0 };
384: 
385:   for (const r of results) {
386:     summary.total++;
387:     if (r.status === 'ok') summary.passed++;
388:     else if (r.status === 'fail') summary.failed++;
389:     else if (r.status === 'partial') summary.partial++;
390:   }
391: 
392:   const report: Report = { summary, results };
393:   console.log(JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
394: }
395: 
396: // ── Main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
397: 
398: async function main() {
399:   const { entries: cliEntries, filePath } = parseArgs();
400:   let allEntries: SourceEntry[] = [...cliEntries];
401: 
402:   if (filePath) {
403:     try {
404:       const fileEntries = await loadSourcesFromYaml(filePath);
405:       allEntries = allEntries.concat(fileEntries);
406:     } catch (err: any) {
407:       console.error(`Error loading YAML file "${filePath}": ${err.message}`);
408:       process.exit(1);
409:     }
410:   }
411: 
412:   if (allEntries.length === 0) {
413:     console.error('No sources to validate. Provide --url/--parser pairs or --file.');
414:     console.error('');
415:     console.error('Usage:');
416:     console.error('  bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>');
417:     console.error('  bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>');
418:     process.exit(1);
419:   }
420: 
421:   const results: ValidationResult[] = [];
422: 
423:   for (let i = 0; i < allEntries.length; i++) {
424:     const entry = allEntries[i];
425:     const label = entry.name || entry.url;
426:     process.stderr.write(`[${i + 1}/${allEntries.length}] ${label}... `);
427: 
428:     const result = await validateSource(entry);
429:     results.push(result);
430: 
431:     const icons: Record<string, string> = { ok: '✓', fail: '✗', partial: '~' };
432:     process.stderr.write(`${icons[result.status] || '?'} ${result.status}`);
433:     if (result.items_count > 0) process.stderr.write(` (${result.items_count} items)`);
434:     if (result.error) process.stderr.write(` — ${result.error.slice(0, 80)}`);
435:     process.stderr.write('\n');
436:   }
437: 
438:   printReport(results);
439: 
440:   const anyFailed = results.some(r => r.status === 'fail');
441:   process.exit(anyFailed ? 1 : 0);
442: }
443: 
444: main().catch(err => {
445:   console.error('Fatal error:', err);
446:   process.exit(1);
447: });

</content>

1: #!/usr/bin/env bun
2: /**
3:  * health-monitor.ts — Health monitoring for all sources in sources.yml
4:  *
5:  * Periodically checks all sources and reports which are healthy, failing, or stale.
6:  * Can be run standalone: bun run scripts/health-monitor.ts
7:  *
8:  * Exit code:
9:  *   0 — All sources healthy (no failures)
10:  *   1 — One or more sources are failing
11:  */
12: 
13: import { request } from 'undici';
14: import yaml from 'js-yaml';
15: import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
16: import path from 'node:path';
17: 
18: // ── Types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19: 
20: interface SourceEntry {
21:   id: string;
22:   name: string;
23:   type: string;
24:   url?: string;
25:   platform?: string;
26:   parser?: string;
27:   is_active?: boolean;
28:   rate_limit?: { interval_seconds?: number; batch_size?: number };
29:   [key: string]: unknown;
30: }
31: 
32: interface HealthResult {
33:   timestamp: string;
34:   summary: {
35:     total: number;
36:     healthy: number;
37:     failing: number;
38:     stale: number;
39:     inactive: number;
40:   };
41:   sources: Array<{
42:     id: string;
43:     name: string;
44:     status: 'healthy' | 'failing' | 'stale' | 'inactive';
45:     responseTime?: number;
46:     error?: string;
47:     sourceType?: string;
48:   }>;
49: }
50: 
51: type SourceStatus = 'healthy' | 'failing' | 'stale' | 'inactive';
52: 
53: // ── Config resolution ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54: 
55: function resolveConfigPath(): string {
56:   const envDir = process.env.IGS_CONFIG_DIR;
57:   if (envDir) {
58:     return path.join(envDir, 'sources.yml');
59:   }
60:   // Default: look relative to script location
61:   const scriptDir = path.dirname(import.meta.path);
62:   const repoRoot = path.resolve(scriptDir, '..');
63:   return path.join(repoRoot, 'config', 'sources.yml');
64: }
65: 
66: // ── Health check for RSS/HTTP sources ─────────────────────────────────────────
67: 
68: interface CheckResult {
69:   status: SourceStatus;
70:   responseTime?: number;
71:   error?: string;
72: }
73: 
74: async function checkHttpSource(source: SourceEntry): Promise<CheckResult> {
75:   const url = source.url;
76:   if (!url) {
77:     return { status: 'failing', error: 'No URL configured' };
78:   }
79: 
80:   const startTime = performance.now();
81: 
82:   try {
83:     const resp = await request(url, {
84:       method: 'GET',
85:       headers: {
86:         'User-Agent': 'IGS-HealthMonitor/1.0 (+https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp)',
87:         Accept: 'application/rss+xml, application/json, text/html, application/xml, */*',
88:       },
89:       headersTimeout: 10_000,
90:       bodyTimeout: 5_000,
91:     });
92: 
93:     const elapsed = performance.now() - startTime;
94:     const elapsedSeconds = elapsed / 1000;
95: 
96:     if (resp.statusCode !== 200) {
97:       return { status: 'failing', responseTime: elapsedSeconds, error: `HTTP ${resp.statusCode}` };
98:     }
99: 
100:     // Check if body is non-empty for confidence
101:     const contentLength = resp.headers['content-length'];
102:     if (contentLength !== undefined && contentLength !== null) {
103:       const length = typeof contentLength === 'string' ? parseInt(contentLength, 10) : Number(contentLength);
104:       if (length === 0) {
105:         return { status: 'stale', responseTime: elapsedSeconds, error: 'Content-Length is 0' };
106:       }
107:     }
108: 
109:     if (elapsedSeconds < 3) {
110:       return { status: 'healthy', responseTime: elapsedSeconds };
111:     } else if (elapsedSeconds <= 10) {
112:       return { status: 'stale', responseTime: elapsedSeconds, error: `Slow response (${elapsedSeconds.toFixed(1)}s)` };
113:     } else {
114:       // Shouldn't reach here with the 10s timeout, but just in case
115:       return { status: 'stale', responseTime: elapsedSeconds, error: `Response time ${elapsedSeconds.toFixed(1)}s exceeds 10s threshold` };
116:     }
117:   } catch (err: any) {
118:     const elapsed = performance.now() - startTime;
119:     const elapsedSeconds = elapsed / 1000;
120: 
121:     let errorMsg: string;
122:     if (err.name === 'AbortError' || err.type === 'aborted' || err.message?.includes('abort')) {
123:       errorMsg = 'timeout (10s)';
124:     } else if (err.code === 'ENOTFOUND') {
125:       errorMsg = `DNS resolution failed`;
126:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNREFUSED') {
127:       errorMsg = `Connection refused`;
128:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNRESET') {
129:       errorMsg = `Connection reset`;
130:     } else if (err.code === 'UND_ERR_HEADERS_TIMEOUT') {
131:       errorMsg = 'Headers timeout';
132:     } else if (err.code === 'UND_ERR_BODY_TIMEOUT') {
133:       errorMsg = 'Body timeout';
134:     } else {
135:       errorMsg = err.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(err);
136:     }
137: 
138:     return { status: 'failing', responseTime: elapsedSeconds, error: errorMsg };
139:   }
140: }
141: 
142: // ── Health check for social_media sources ────────────────────────────────────
143: 
144: const VALID_PLATFORMS = new Set(['reddit', 'twitter']);
145: 
146: function checkSocialMediaSource(source: SourceEntry): CheckResult {
147:   const platform = source.platform;
148: 
149:   if (!platform) {
150:     return { status: 'failing', error: 'Missing platform field' };
151:   }
152: 
153:   if (!VALID_PLATFORMS.has(platform)) {
154:     return { status: 'failing', error: `Unknown platform: "${platform}"` };
155:   }
156: 
157:   // Check rate_limit config completeness
158:   const rateLimit = source.rate_limit;
159:   if (!rateLimit) {
160:     return { status: 'stale', error: 'Missing rate_limit config' };
161:   }
162: 
163:   if (typeof rateLimit.interval_seconds !== 'number' || typeof rateLimit.batch_size !== 'number') {
164:     return { status: 'stale', error: 'Incomplete rate_limit config (needs interval_seconds and batch_size)' };
165:   }
166: 
167:   return { status: 'healthy' };
168: }
169: 
170: // ── Main health check ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
171: 
172: async function checkSource(source: SourceEntry): Promise<{
173:   status: SourceStatus;
174:   responseTime?: number;
175:   error?: string;
176: }> {
177:   // Inactive sources are excluded from active count
178:   if (source.is_active === false) {
179:     return { status: 'inactive' };
180:   }
181: 
182:   switch (source.type) {
183:     case 'rss':
184:     case 'http':
185:       return checkHttpSource(source);
186: 
187:     case 'social_media':
188:       return checkSocialMediaSource(source);
189: 
190:     default:
191:       return { status: 'stale', error: `Unknown source type: "${source.type}"` };
192:   }
193: }
194: 
195: // ── Main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
196: 
197: async function main() {
198:   const configPath = resolveConfigPath();
199: 
200:   let sourcesYaml: string;
201:   try {
202:     sourcesYaml = await fs.readFile(configPath, 'utf-8');
203:   } catch (err: any) {
204:     console.error(`Error reading config file "${configPath}": ${err.message}`);
205:     process.exit(1);
206:   }
207: 
208:   let data: any;
209:   try {
210:     data = yaml.load(sourcesYaml);
211:   } catch (err: any) {
212:     console.error(`Error parsing YAML: ${err.message}`);
213:     process.exit(1);
214:   }
215: 
216:   if (!data || !Array.isArray(data.sources)) {
217:     console.error('sources.yml must contain a "sources" array');
218:     process.exit(1);
219:   }
220: 
221:   const sources: SourceEntry[] = data.sources;
222:   const results: HealthResult['sources'] = [];
223: 
224:   for (let i = 0; i < sources.length; i++) {
225:     const source = sources[i];
226:     const label = `[${i + 1}/${sources.length}] ${source.name || source.id}`;
227:     process.stderr.write(`${label}... `);
228: 
229:     const check = await checkSource(source);
230: 
231:     results.push({
232:       id: source.id || `unknown-${i}`,
233:       name: source.name || source.id || `Source ${i}`,
234:       status: check.status,
235:       responseTime: check.responseTime,
236:       error: check.error,
237:       sourceType: source.type,
238:     });
239: 
240:     const icons: Record<string, string> = {
241:       healthy: '✓',
242:       failing: '✗',
243:       stale: '~',
244:       inactive: '-',
245:     };
246:     process.stderr.write(`${icons[check.status] || '?'} ${check.status}`);
247:     if (check.responseTime !== undefined) {
248:       process.stderr.write(` (${check.responseTime.toFixed(1)}s)`);
249:     }
250:     if (check.error) {
251:       process.stderr.write(` — ${check.error.slice(0, 100)}`);
252:     }
253:     process.stderr.write('\n');
254:   }
255: 
256:   // Build summary — inactive are not counted in "total"
257:   const activeSources = results.filter(r => r.status !== 'inactive');
258:   const summary = {
259:     total: activeSources.length,
260:     healthy: results.filter(r => r.status === 'healthy').length,
261:     failing: results.filter(r => r.status === 'failing').length,
262:     stale: results.filter(r => r.status === 'stale').length,
263:     inactive: results.filter(r => r.status === 'inactive').length,
264:   };
265: 
266:   const report: HealthResult = {
267:     timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
268:     summary,
269:     sources: results,
270:   };
271: 
272:   console.log(JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
273: 
274:   const anyFailing = results.some(r => r.status === 'failing');
275:   process.exit(anyFailing ? 1 : 0);
276: }
277: 
278: main().catch(err => {
279:   console.error('Fatal error:', err);
280:   process.exit(1);
281: });

341: // CHECK 6: Tier Values — only 1, 2, or 3 if present

343:

357: 
358:   for (let i = 0; i < sources.length; i++) {
359:     const src = sources[i];
360:     if (!src) continue;
361: 
362:     if (src.tier !== undefined && src.tier !== null) {
363:       tieredCount++;
364:       if (!VALID_TIERS.has(src.tier)) {
365:         fail(check, `Source "${src.id || `#${i}`}" has invalid tier value: ${src.tier} (must be 1, 2, or 3)`);
366:       }
367:     }
368:   }
369: 
370:   check.warnings.push(`${tieredCount}/${sources.length} sources have tier values`);
371:   if (check.errors.length === 0) {
372:     check.warnings.push('All tier values are valid (1, 2, or 3)');
373:   }
374: 
375:   return check;
376: }
377: 
378: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
379: // CHECK 7: Rate Limits — every social_media source has rate_limit config
380: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
381: 
382: async function checkRateLimits(yamlFiles: YamlFile[]): Promise<ValidationCheck> {
383:   const check = makeCheck('Rate Limits (social_media)');
384: 
385:   const sourcesFile = yamlFiles.find(f => f.name === 'sources.yml');
386:   if (!sourcesFile || sourcesFile.error) {
387:     fail(check, 'sources.yml not available');
388:     return check;
389:   }
390: 
391:   const doc = sourcesFile.data as any;
392:   const sources: any[] = doc?.sources ?? [];
393:   let socialCount = 0;
394:   let missingRateLimit = 0;
395: 
396:   for (let i = 0; i < sources.length; i++) {
397:     const src = sources[i];
398:     if (!src) continue;
399: 
400:     if (src.type === 'social_media') {
401:       socialCount++;
402: 
403:       // Must have rate_limit object
404:       if (!src.rate_limit || typeof src.rate_limit !== 'object') {
405:         fail(check, `social_media source "${src.id || `#${i}`}" is missing "rate_limit"`);
406:         missingRateLimit++;
407:         continue;
408:       }
409: 
410:       // interval_seconds must be positive
411:       if (!src.rate_limit.interval_seconds || typeof src.rate_limit.interval_seconds !== 'number' || src.rate_limit.interval_seconds < 1) {
412:         fail(check, `social_media source "${src.id || `#${i}`}" has invalid or missing rate_limit.interval_seconds`);
413:       }
414: 
415:       // batch_size should be positive if present
416:       if (src.rate_limit.batch_size !== undefined && (typeof src.rate_limit.batch_size !== 'number' || src.rate_limit.batch_size < 1)) {
417:         fail(check, `social_media source "${src.id || `#${i}`}" has invalid rate_limit.batch_size (must be positive integer)`);
418:       }
419:     }
420:   }
421: 
422:   if (socialCount === 0) {
423:     warn(check, 'No social_media sources found — rate limit check skipped');
424:   } else if (missingRateLimit === 0) {
425:     check.warnings.push(`All ${socialCount} social_media sources have valid rate_limit config`);
426:   }
427: 
428:   return check;
429: }
430: 
431: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
432: // CHECK 8: URL Format — all non-social_media sources have valid, parseable URLs
433: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
434: 
435: async function checkUrlFormat(yamlFiles: YamlFile[]): Promise<ValidationCheck> {
436:   const check = makeCheck('URL Format');
437: 
438:   const sourcesFile = yamlFiles.find(f => f.name === 'sources.yml');
439:   if (!sourcesFile || sourcesFile.error) {
440:     fail(check, 'sources.yml not available');
441:     return check;
442:   }
443: 
444:   const doc = sourcesFile.data as any;
445:   const sources: any[] = doc?.sources ?? [];
446:   let checkedCount = 0;
447:   let skippedCount = 0;
448: 
449:   for (let i = 0; i < sources.length; i++) {
450:     const src = sources[i];
451:     if (!src) continue;
452: 
453:     // Skip social_media sources — they use identifiers, not URLs
454:     if (src.type === 'social_media') {
455:       skippedCount++;
456:       continue;
457:     }
458: 
459:     // Must have a url field
460:     if (!src.url || typeof src.url !== 'string' || src.url.trim() === '') {
461:       fail(check, `Source "${src.id || `#${i}`}" (type: ${src.type || 'unknown'}) has missing or empty "url"`);
462:       continue;
463:     }
464: 
465:     // Must be parseable as URL
466:     checkedCount++;
467:     try {
468:       new URL(src.url);
469:     } catch {
470:       fail(check, `Source "${src.id || `#${i}`}" has invalid URL: "${src.url}"`);
471:     }
472:   }
473: 
474:   if (check.errors.length === 0) {
475:     check.warnings.push(`All ${checkedCount} non-social_media sources have valid URLs (${skippedCount} social_media sources skipped)`);
476:   }
477: 
478:   return check;
479: }
480: 
481: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
482: // REPORTING
483: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
484: 
485: function printReport(report: ValidationReport): void {
486:   // Summary line (stderr) for human reading
487:   const icon = report.failed === 0 ? '✅' : '❌';
488:   process.stderr.write(`\n${icon} Full Validation Report\n`);
489:   process.stderr.write(`   Timestamp: ${report.timestamp}\n`);
490:   process.stderr.write(`   Checks:    ${report.totalChecks} total, ${report.passed} passed, ${report.failed} failed\n\n`);
491: 
492:   // Detailed output per check
493:   for (const check of report.checks) {
494:     const status = check.passed ? '✅ PASS' : '❌ FAIL';
495:     process.stderr.write(`  ${status}  ${check.name}\n`);
496: 
497:     if (check.warnings.length > 0) {
498:       for (const w of check.warnings) {
499:         process.stderr.write(`       ⚠  ${w}\n`);
500:       }
501:     }
502: 
503:     if (check.errors.length > 0) {
504:       for (const e of check.errors) {
505:         process.stderr.write(`       ✗  ${e}\n`);
506:       }
507:     }
508:     process.stderr.write('\n');
509:   }
510: 
511:   // JSON output (stdout) for programmatic consumption
512:   console.log(JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
513: }
514: 
515: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
516: // MAIN
517: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
518: 
519: async function main(): Promise<void> {
520:   // Load all YAML files once
521:   const yamlFiles = await loadYamlFiles();
522: 
523:   // Run all checks
524:   const checks = await Promise.all([
525:     checkYamlSyntax(yamlFiles),
526:     checkSchema(yamlFiles),
527:     checkSourceIds(yamlFiles),
528:     checkPoolReferences(yamlFiles),
529:     checkRequiredFields(yamlFiles),
530:     checkTierValues(yamlFiles),
531:     checkRateLimits(yamlFiles),
532:     checkUrlFormat(yamlFiles),
533:   ]);
534: 
535:   const report = makeReport(checks);
536:   printReport(report);
537: 
538:   process.exit(report.failed > 0 ? 1 : 0);
539: }
540: 
541: main().catch(err => {

544: });

 * Runs ALL of the following checks:
