<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bun
2: /**
3:  * performance-test.ts — Performance benchmark for source fetch throughput,
4:  * parse latency, and cache efficiency.
5:  *
6:  * Tests 3 scenarios:
7:  *   1. Single source baseline: 1 RSS + 1 social_media source
8:  *   2. Batch throughput: 10 RSS sources concurrently
9:  *   3. Cache efficiency: Same RSS source fetched twice
10:  *
11:  * Usage:
12:  *   bun run scripts/performance-test.ts
13:  *
14:  * Output: JSON report to stdout, progress to stderr.
15:  * Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on errors.
16:  *
17:  * Safe: read-only, no state mutation, uses existing configs.
18:  */
19: 
20: import { request } from 'undici';
21: import yaml from 'js-yaml';
22: import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
23: import path from 'node:path';
24: 
25: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
26: // Types
27: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
28: 
29: interface SourceConfig {
30:   id: string;
31:   name: string;
32:   type: string;
33:   url?: string;
34:   parser?: string;
35:   platform?: string;
36:   is_active?: boolean;
37:   [key: string]: unknown;
38: }
39: 
40: interface PerfDetail {
41:   id: string;
42:   name: string;
43:   type: string;
44:   duration_ms: number;
45:   status: 'success' | 'skipped' | 'error';
46:   byte_length?: number;
47:   error?: string;
48: }
49: 
50: interface PerfResult {
51:   test: string;
52:   description: string;
53:   duration_ms: number;
54:   sources_tested: number;
55:   sources_succeeded: number;
56:   avg_latency_ms: number;
57:   errors: string[];
58:   details: PerfDetail[];
59: }
60: 
61: interface Report {
62:   timestamp: string;
63:   total_duration_ms: number;
64:   results: PerfResult[];
65:   summary: {
66:     tests_run: number;
67:     tests_passed: number;
68:     total_sources_tested: number;
69:     total_sources_succeeded: number;
70:     total_errors: number;
71:   };
72: }
73: 
74: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
75: // Helpers
76: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
77: 
78: function resolveConfigDir(): string {
79:   return process.env.IGS_CONFIG_DIR
80:     || path.join(process.env.HOME || '/tmp', '.config', 'igs-mcp');
81: }
82: 
83: async function loadSources(): Promise<SourceConfig[]> {
84:   // Try user config first, fall back to project config
85:   const candidates = [
86:     path.join(resolveConfigDir(), 'sources.yml'),
87:     path.join(import.meta.dirname, '..', 'config', 'sources.yml'),
88:   ];
89: 
90:   let raw = '';
91:   for (const p of candidates) {
92:     try {
93:       raw = await fs.readFile(p, 'utf-8');
94:       break;
95:     } catch {
96:       continue;
97:     }
98:   }
99:   if (!raw) throw new Error('Could not find sources.yml in any config path');
100: 
101:   const data = yaml.load(raw) as any;
102:   if (!data || !Array.isArray(data.sources)) {
103:     throw new Error('sources.yml must have a "sources" key with an array');
104:   }
105:   return data.sources;
106: }
107: 
108: async function fetchUrl(
109:   url: string,
110:   timeoutMs = 15_000,
111: ): Promise<{ body: string; byteLength: number; durationMs: number }> {
112:   const start = performance.now();
113:   const controller = new AbortController();
114:   const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
115: 
116:   try {
117:     const resp = await request(url, {
118:       method: 'GET',
119:       headers: {
120:         'User-Agent': 'IGS-PerfTest/1.0 (+https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp)',
121:         Accept: 'application/rss+xml, application/json, text/html, application/xml, */*',
122:       },
123:       signal: controller.signal,
124:     });
125: 
126:     clearTimeout(timer);
127: 
128:     const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
129:     for await (const chunk of resp.body) {
130:       chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(chunk));
131:     }
132:     const body = Buffer.concat(chunks).toString('utf-8');
133:     const durationMs = performance.now() - start;
134: 
135:     return { body, byteLength: body.length, durationMs };
136:   } catch (err: unknown) {
137:     clearTimeout(timer);
138:     throw err;
139:   }
140: }
141: 
142: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
143: // Test 1: Single source baseline
144: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
145: 
146: async function testBaseline(sources: SourceConfig[]): Promise<PerfResult> {
147:   const errors: string[] = [];
148:   const details: PerfDetail[] = [];
149: 
150:   const rssSrc = sources.find(s => s.type === 'rss' && s.is_active !== false && s.url);
151:   const socialSrc = sources.find(s => s.type === 'social_media' && s.is_active !== false);
152: 
153:   const candidates = [
154:     { src: rssSrc, label: 'RSS' },
155:     { src: socialSrc, label: 'social_media' },
156:   ].filter(c => c.src != null) as { src: SourceConfig; label: string }[];
157: 
158:   const testStart = performance.now();
159: 
160:   for (const { src } of candidates) {
161:     if (src.type === 'rss' || src.type === 'http') {
162:       try {
163:         const result = await fetchUrl(src.url!, 15_000);
164:         details.push({
165:           id: src.id,
166:           name: src.name || src.id,
167:           type: src.type,
168:           duration_ms: Math.round(result.durationMs),
169:           status: 'success',
170:           byte_length: result.byteLength,
171:         });
172:         process.stderr.write(`  [ok]  ${src.id}  ${Math.round(result.durationMs)}ms  ${result.byteLength}b\n`);
173:       } catch (err: unknown) {
174:         const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message.slice(0, 200) : String(err);
175:         details.push({
176:           id: src.id,
177:           name: src.name || src.id,
178:           type: src.type,
179:           duration_ms: Math.round(performance.now() - testStart),
180:           status: 'error',
181:           error: msg,
182:         });
183:         errors.push(`${src.id}: ${msg}`);
184:         process.stderr.write(`  [err] ${src.id}  — ${msg}\n`);
185:       }
186:     } else if (src.type === 'social_media') {
187:       // Social media sources require auth / platform API — not callable from a
188:       // standalone script. Record config-lookup time and skip actual fetch.
189:       details.push({
190:         id: src.id,
191:         name: src.name || src.id,
192:         type: src.type,
193:         duration_ms: 0,
194:         status: 'skipped',
195:         error: 'social_media requires platform auth — config lookup only',
196:       });
197:       process.stderr.write(`  [—]  ${src.id}  skipped (social_media needs auth)\n`);
198:     }
199:   }
200: 
201:   const totalDuration = performance.now() - testStart;
202:   const succeeded = details.filter(d => d.status === 'success').length;
203:   const successDurations = details.filter(d => d.status === 'success').map(d => d.duration_ms);
204:   const avgLatency = successDurations.length
205:     ? Math.round(successDurations.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / successDurations.length)
206:     : 0;
207: 
208:   return {
209:     test: 'single_source_baseline',
210:     description: 'Fetch 1 RSS source and 1 social_media source — measures raw latency per type',
211:     duration_ms: Math.round(totalDuration),
212:     sources_tested: details.length,
213:     sources_succeeded: succeeded,
214:     avg_latency_ms: avgLatency,
215:     errors,
216:     details,
217:   };
218: }
219: 
220: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
221: // Test 2: Batch throughput
222: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
223: 
224: async function testBatch(sources: SourceConfig[]): Promise<PerfResult> {
225:   const errors: string[] = [];
226:   const details: PerfDetail[] = [];
227: 
228:   const batch = sources
229:     .filter(s => s.type === 'rss' && s.is_active !== false && s.url)
230:     .slice(0, 10);
231: 
232:   if (batch.length === 0) {
233:     return {
234:       test: 'batch_fetch',
235:       description: 'Fetch 10 RSS sources concurrently — measures throughput',
236:       duration_ms: 0,
237:       sources_tested: 0,
238:       sources_succeeded: 0,
239:       avg_latency_ms: 0,
240:       errors: ['No active RSS sources available for batch test'],
241:       details: [],
242:     };
243:   }
244: 
245:   process.stderr.write(`  batch: ${batch.length} sources\n`);
246: 
247:   const batchStart = performance.now();
248:   const settled = await Promise.allSettled(
249:     batch.map(async (src) => {
250:       const itemStart = performance.now();
251:       try {
252:         const result = await fetchUrl(src.url!, 30_000);
253:         const elapsed = Math.round(performance.now() - itemStart);
254:         process.stderr.write(`    [ok]  ${src.id}  ${elapsed}ms  ${result.byteLength}b\n`);
255:         return {
256:           id: src.id,
257:           name: src.name || src.id,
258:           type: src.type,
259:           duration_ms: elapsed,
260:           status: 'success' as const,
261:           byte_length: result.byteLength,
262:         };
263:       } catch (err: unknown) {
264:         const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message.slice(0, 200) : String(err);
265:         process.stderr.write(`    [err] ${src.id}  — ${msg}\n`);
266:         return {
267:           id: src.id,
268:           name: src.name || src.id,
269:           type: src.type,
270:           duration_ms: Math.round(performance.now() - itemStart),
271:           status: 'error' as const,
272:           error: msg,
273:         };
274:       }
275:     }),
276:   );
277: 
278:   for (const r of settled) {
279:     if (r.status === 'fulfilled') {
280:       details.push(r.value);
281:       if (r.value.status === 'error') errors.push(`${r.value.id}: ${r.value.error}`);
282:     }
283:   }
284: 
285:   const totalDuration = performance.now() - batchStart;
286:   const succeeded = details.filter(d => d.status === 'success').length;
287:   const successDurations = details.filter(d => d.status === 'success').map(d => d.duration_ms);
288:   const avgLatency = successDurations.length
289:     ? Math.round(successDurations.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / successDurations.length)
290:     : 0;
291: 
292:   return {
293:     test: 'batch_fetch',
294:     description: `Fetch ${batch.length} RSS sources concurrently with Promise.allSettled`,
295:     duration_ms: Math.round(totalDuration),
296:     sources_tested: batch.length,
297:     sources_succeeded: succeeded,
298:     avg_latency_ms: avgLatency,
299:     errors,
300:     details,
301:   };
302: }
303: 
304: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
305: // Test 3: Cache efficiency
306: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
307: 
308: async function testCache(sources: SourceConfig[]): Promise<PerfResult> {
309:   const errors: string[] = [];
310:   const details: PerfDetail[] = [];
311: 
312:   const cacheSrc = sources.find(s => s.type === 'rss' && s.is_active !== false && s.url);
313:   if (!cacheSrc) {
314:     return {
315:       test: 'cache_efficiency',
316:       description: 'Fetch same RSS source twice — measures cache efficiency (first vs second)',
317:       duration_ms: 0,
318:       sources_tested: 0,
319:       sources_succeeded: 0,
320:       avg_latency_ms: 0,
321:       errors: ['No active RSS source available for cache test'],
322:       details: [],
323:     };
324:   }
325: 
326:   process.stderr.write(`  source: ${cacheSrc.id}\n`);
327:   const testStart = performance.now();
328: 
329:   // ── First fetch (cold) ─────────────────────────────────────────
330:   try {
331:     const first = await fetchUrl(cacheSrc.url!, 15_000);
332:     details.push({
333:       id: `${cacheSrc.id}#1`,
334:       name: `${cacheSrc.name} (first fetch)`,
335:       type: cacheSrc.type,
336:       duration_ms: Math.round(first.durationMs),
337:       status: 'success',
338:       byte_length: first.byteLength,
339:     });
340:     process.stderr.write(`    [1] ${cacheSrc.id}  ${Math.round(first.durationMs)}ms  ${first.byteLength}b\n`);
341: 
342:     if (first.byteLength === 0) {
343:       errors.push(`${cacheSrc.id}: empty body on first fetch`);
344:     }
345:   } catch (err: unknown) {
346:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message.slice(0, 200) : String(err);
347:     details.push({
348:       id: `${cacheSrc.id}#1`,
349:       name: `${cacheSrc.name} (first fetch)`,
350:       type: cacheSrc.type,
351:       duration_ms: Math.round(performance.now() - testStart),
352:       status: 'error',
353:       error: msg,
354:     });
355:     errors.push(`${cacheSrc.id}: first fetch failed — ${msg}`);
356:   }
357: 
358:   // ── Second fetch (hot — may hit HTTP cache / ETag) ─────────────
359:   try {
360:     const second = await fetchUrl(cacheSrc.url!, 15_000);
361:     details.push({
362:       id: `${cacheSrc.id}#2`,
363:       name: `${cacheSrc.name} (second fetch)`,
364:       type: cacheSrc.type,
365:       duration_ms: Math.round(second.durationMs),
366:       status: 'success',
367:       byte_length: second.byteLength,
368:     });
369:     process.stderr.write(`    [2] ${cacheSrc.id}  ${Math.round(second.durationMs)}ms  ${second.byteLength}b\n`);
370:   } catch (err: unknown) {
371:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message.slice(0, 200) : String(err);
372:     details.push({
373:       id: `${cacheSrc.id}#2`,
374:       name: `${cacheSrc.name} (second fetch)`,
375:       type: cacheSrc.type,
376:       duration_ms: Math.round(performance.now() - testStart),
377:       status: 'error',
378:       error: msg,
379:     });
380:     errors.push(`${cacheSrc.id}: second fetch failed — ${msg}`);
381:   }
382: 
383:   const totalDuration = performance.now() - testStart;
384:   const succeeded = details.filter(d => d.status === 'success').length;
385:   const successDurations = details.filter(d => d.status === 'success').map(d => d.duration_ms);
386:   const avgLatency = successDurations.length
387:     ? Math.round(successDurations.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / successDurations.length)
388:     : 0;
389: 
390:   return {
391:     test: 'cache_efficiency',
392:     description: 'Fetch same RSS source twice — measures cache efficiency (first vs second)',
393:     duration_ms: Math.round(totalDuration),
394:     sources_tested: details.length,
395:     sources_succeeded: succeeded,
396:     avg_latency_ms: avgLatency,
397:     errors,
398:     details,
399:   };
400: }
401: 
402: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
403: // Main
404: // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
405: 
406: async function main() {
407:   const globalStart = performance.now();
408: 
409:   process.stderr.write('Loading sources...\n');
410:   let sources: SourceConfig[];
411:   try {
412:     sources = await loadSources();
413:   } catch (err: unknown) {
414:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
415:     process.stderr.write(`FATAL: ${msg}\n`);
416:     process.exit(1);
417:   }
418: 
419:   const active = sources.filter(s => s.is_active !== false);
420:   process.stderr.write(`Loaded ${sources.length} sources (${active.length} active)\n\n`);
421: 
422:   // ── Test 1: Baseline ───────────────────────────────────────────
423:   process.stderr.write('── Test 1: Single source baseline ──\n');
424:   const baseline = await testBaseline(sources);
425:   process.stderr.write(`  done: ${baseline.duration_ms}ms\n\n`);
426: 
427:   // ── Test 2: Batch ──────────────────────────────────────────────
428:   process.stderr.write('── Test 2: Batch fetch ──\n');
429:   const batch = await testBatch(sources);
430:   process.stderr.write(`  done: ${batch.duration_ms}ms\n\n`);
431: 
432:   // ── Test 3: Cache ──────────────────────────────────────────────
433:   process.stderr.write('── Test 3: Cache efficiency ──\n');
434:   const cache = await testCache(sources);
435:   process.stderr.write(`  done: ${cache.duration_ms}ms\n\n`);
436: 
437:   // ── Report ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
438:   const totalDuration = performance.now() - globalStart;
439:   const tests = [baseline, batch, cache];
440:   const testsPassed = tests.filter(t =>
441:     t.errors.length === 0 || t.details.some(d => d.status === 'success'),
442:   );
443: 
444:   const report: Report = {
445:     timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
446:     total_duration_ms: Math.round(totalDuration),
447:     results: tests,
448:     summary: {
449:       tests_run: tests.length,
450:       tests_passed: testsPassed.length,
451:       total_sources_tested: tests.reduce((s, t) => s + t.sources_tested, 0),
452:       total_sources_succeeded: tests.reduce((s, t) => s + t.sources_succeeded, 0),
453:       total_errors: tests.reduce((s, t) => s + t.errors.length, 0),
454:     },
455:   };
456: 
457:   // JSON report to stdout (pipeline-friendly)
458:   console.log(JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
459: 
460:   // Exit 1 only if ALL tests failed entirely
461:   const allFailed = tests.every(t => t.sources_succeeded === 0 && t.sources_tested > 0);
462:   const tooManyErrors = report.summary.total_errors > report.summary.total_sources_tested * 0.5;
463:   process.exit(allFailed || tooManyErrors ? 1 : 0);
464: }
465: 
466: main().catch((err: unknown) => {
467:   const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
468:   process.stderr.write(`FATAL: ${msg}\n`);
469:   console.log(JSON.stringify({ fatal: msg }, null, 2));
470:   process.exit(1);
471: });
